new_classes.label_en: Other Sparkling Wine with High Excess Pressure (CN 22041096)
new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine of fresh grapes, other than Champagne, Prosecco, and other sparkling wine classified under 22041094, with an excess pressure of 3 bar or more at 20 °C, classified under CN subheading 2204 10 96 (Chapter 22).

coverage_explanation: The legal note for 22041096 is terse, referencing only "Schaumwein" (sparkling wine) and the range 22041011–22041098, together with Subheading Note 1 to Chapter 22. From the CN structure, 22041096 is the residual "other" sparkling wine subheading (not Champagne 22041011, not Prosecco 22041093, not 22041094) with an excess pressure of ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C. The axioms capture: (1) membership in SparklingWine, (2) the CN subheading code hasValue, (3) the ≥ 3 bar excess pressure threshold, and (4) exclusion of Champagne-region and Prosecco/Glera-variety origin qualities. Coverage is partial (0.72) because: (a) the full content of Subheading Note 1 (which defines "sparkling wine" by pressure criteria) is referenced but not reproduced here; (b) the precise boundary with 22041094 (which covers wines not meeting the ≥ 3 bar threshold) cannot be fully expressed without a complementary upper-bound restriction on 22041094; and (c) the legal text's cross-reference to Subheading Note 1 implies additional quality/appellation distinctions that cannot be exhaustively enumerated without that full note text.
